what_it_does: A static HTML/CSS design mockup of a consolidated Settings panel reimagined as a dark, aurora-backed 'living instrument' with a section rail, segmented resource-fit meter, glowing brain-model slot, fold-away advanced knobs, and selectable persona cards.
observations: Single-file HTML/CSS mockup with no JavaScript, no backend, and no registered code symbols.
Uses Fraunces, IBM Plex Mono, and IBM Plex Sans loaded from Google Fonts; dark theme driven by CSS custom properties.
Header comment explicitly maps each control to Settings.tsx line ranges and labels the file as an exploration mockup, not a corpus addition.
Key interactive elements carry data-ui-ref attributes such as ui://settings/brain-persona and ui://settings/resource-fit.
Navigation rail shows five sections: Brain & persona, Modes, Voice, Model roles, Composition.
Resource-fit meter displays memory breakdown as colored segments with an honest 'fits the card' verdict.
Brain model is represented as a pulsing glowing core with model name, local/loaded status, and a swap button.
Advanced parameters (temperature, max tokens, context window, timeout, endpoint) are hidden inside a <details> progressive-disclosure fold.
Persona selection is shown as three face cards with active state, voice engine labels, and short descriptions.
All animations and states are pure CSS; no dynamic data binding or event handling is present.
standouts: The 'living instrument' direction replaces conventional form rows with tactile controls: a slot, a meter, and a cast of cards.
Progressive disclosure of advanced knobs via a native <details> element directly addresses the stated complaint about form-row density.
data-ui-ref annotations create an explicit reference layer between visual elements and conceptual settings domains.
The header explicitly disclaims registration/corpus status while still anchoring the mockup to source ranges.
conventions: Follows a design-token approach with CSS custom properties for color, typography, and spacing.
Uses semantic HTML elements: nav, button, details/summary.
Breaks the typical settings-form convention by avoiding labeled input rows in favor of cards, meters, and slots.
Uses inline style widths for meter segments rather than dynamic classes or attributes.
Uses emoji and symbolic glyphs as navigation icons without textual fallbacks.
concerns: All data (memory usage, model name, personas, endpoint) is hard-coded with no binding or update mechanism.
Inline style widths on the meter segments are brittle for dynamic resource values.
Active states in the rail and persona cards lack ARIA attributes such as aria-current, aria-pressed, or aria-expanded.
Emoji icons in the rail may render inconsistently and lack accessible text alternatives.
Because the file is marked as an unregistered exploration mockup, it risks becoming orphaned from the implemented Settings surface.
notes: A high-fidelity visual exploration of a settings surface that trades form rows for instrument-like controls, annotated with ui:// reference hooks but lacking interactivity or data binding.
questions: How do the data-ui-ref values connect to runtime UI state, tests, or design-system documentation?
Which parts of this mockup are intended for implementation in Settings.tsx and which are purely conceptual?
Are the memory-fit numbers static placeholders or expected to be computed by a real resource estimator?
Is the rail meant to switch tabbed content, scroll to sections, or is it only decorative in this mockup?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a static, annotated visual mockup exploring an instrument-like reimagining of the consolidated Settings surface.
intention_signals: Reframe a dense settings form as a legible, tactile control surface where the active model, resource budget, persona, and advanced parameters are perceived at a glance through shape, light, and fold-away detail.
novelty: Treats settings as a physical instrument panel—brain slot, fit meter, cast of personas—rather than a conventional preferences form, while explicitly disclaiming corpus status and anchoring to source lines.
contribution: A concrete visual vocabulary and layout for an instrument-style settings surface; a pattern for annotating UI elements with conceptual ui:// references; a progressive-disclosure fold for advanced model parameters; and a segmented resource-fit meter design for communicating memory budget.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS design mockup for a consolidated settings panel styled as a dark, aurora-backed instrument with a section rail, segmented resource-fit meter, glowing brain-model slot, fold-away advanced knobs, and selectable persona cards, annotated with ui:// reference attributes.
intention_for_embedding: Explore a more tactile, less form-row-dense presentation of model, persona, voice, and resource settings for a consolidated settings surface.
